head.hi: The Minister in China (Johnson) to the Secretary of State

div.p:
401. Legation’s 380, April 27, 4 p.m.Not printed. Following from American Consul General at Mukden:
“May 3, 6 p.m. Japanese military now report that negotiations for Nielsen’s release are not progressing satisfactorily and that the commander at Siuyen has decided to resort to military measures at first opportunity.
Owing to the difficulties of communication it seems advisable that Consular officer visit Siuyen which is accessible only by aeroplane. In response to my request the military state that availability of space on military transport planes is extremely uncertain. However, they have offered to assist this office to hire a special plane for the trip which will cost a maximum of about yen 450. Propose sending Hall.Monroe B. Hall, Vice Consul at Mukden. Request authorization of expenditure.”Authorization was given to the Minister In China in telegram No. 146, May 4, 6 p.m. (393.1111 M Nielsen, Niels/12).
